fresh_facts.claim: US Marine boarding attempt on a commercial vessel linked to sanctions evasion was repelled by IRGC boats and coastal warnings in the Strait of Hormuz around April 29, 2026.

US Central Command intercepted the Iranian tanker MT Stream using USS Rafael Peralta as part of a blockade that has stopped approximately 8 tankers and redirected approximately 31 Iran-linked vessels since April 13, 2026.
IRGC gunboats fired on a container ship and cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz around April 29, 2026.
Approximately half of Iran's ballistic missile stockpile and launchers remain intact post-ceasefire as of early April 2026.
Trump issued a 3-day ultimatum on April 28, 2026 warning that Iran's oil pipelines could 'explode from within' while extending ceasefire indefinitely.
US-Iran negotiations mediated by Pakistan in Islamabad collapsed, with Trump canceling a US delegation trip.
ISW's live indicator tracker identifies regime instability signals including senior officials engaging China, Russia, and North Korea for repression aid as of February 3, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Dozens of Iranian security forces were arrested in Kermanshah for refusing to fire on protesters as of February 3, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Khamenei issued an order on January 9, 2026 to crush protests 'by any means necessary'.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Iran deployed approximately 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi militias for protest suppression as of February 3, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: IRGC expressed concerns over defections among its forces as of February 3, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian security forces are framing protesters as 'terrorists' as part of official rhetoric as of February 3, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Recent Iranian protests are assessed as the most serious unrest since 1979, with an official death toll of approximately 2,000 and likely higher.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: An extended internet blackout in Iran included confiscation of Starlink equipment.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Brookings Institution describes Iran's economy as 'collapsing' as of early 2026.
World Bank political stability index for Iran stood at -1.58 in 2024, declining from -1.27 in 2023.
Prediction market odds for Iranian regime fall by June 30, 2026 reached 7.5% YES shares as of April 2026.
Israel struck a pharmaceutical plant in Iran.
US bombers struck Isfahan and conducted over 90 restrikes on Kharg Island according to Pentagon assessment.
IRGC plans attacks on US tech companies in the region.
Thousands more US forces are deploying to the Middle East amid a fragile ceasefire and Iran's renewed attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.
US has inspected or redirected 39 vessels total in its naval campaign in the Strait of Hormuz.
Brookings Institution notes Iran's weakened regional allies including Hezbollah, Hamas, and Assad as of early 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an acute convergence of external military pressure (US naval blockade with 39 vessels inspected/redirected, ongoing Israeli strikes, IRGC attacks on shipping) and the most severe domestic unrest since 1979, with security force defections, ~2,000 confirmed deaths, and regime deployment of foreign militias alongside arrests of officers refusing fire on civilians. Diplomatic channels have collapsed with Trump's cancellation of Pakistan-mediated talks and issuance of a 3-day ultimatum, while the regime's economy deteriorates and regional allies weaken.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Between April 27-29, 2026, Trump canceled US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan and issued a 3-day ultimatum threatening pipeline destruction; IRGC repelled US Marine boarding attempt and fired on commercial vessels in Strait of Hormuz; US deployed additional forces and expanded blockade to 39 vessels; and regime continued militia-led crackdowns on the most serious protests since 1979.
